name: Peter Washington
abstract: Peter Washington  (born August 28, 1964) is a jazz double bassist. He played with the Westchester Community Symphony at the age of 14.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Charlap
about_quotes.evidence: my trio is Kenny Washington and Peter Washington. And we’ve been playing together for the last two and a half years or so.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Charlap interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: For a while it was Mulgrew Miller and Peter Washington and Tony Reedus.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charles McPherson
about_quotes.evidence: That’s “First Flight Out,” Tom Harrell and Peter Washington, Victor Lewis on drums, Michael Weiss on piano and myself
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles McPherson interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Tabackin
about_quotes.evidence: I had a little discussion with a friend of mine, Peter Washington
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Tabackin interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 17, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: It was David Hazeltine on piano, Peter Washington, Jim Rotundi on trumpet, and Ben Riley. I show up and I see Ben Riley... We’re doing “Epistrophy”...
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, March 29, 2015 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Wess
about_quotes.evidence: Peter Washington is doing part of the week and then I have Noreko... Michael Weiss is doing half of the week; he’s usually with me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Frank Wess — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
